title: Incident Response Timeline Milestone Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-008-6 R3 Part 3.2.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-008-6 R3 Part 3.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.40: Incident Response Timeline Milestone Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.40' running on index=itsm sourcetype=incident:milestone or ServiceNow custom sourcetype, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=incident:milestone category="CIP008" earliest=-90d
| eval det=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval con=strptime(contained_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval rec=strptime(recovered_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval contain_h=round((con-det)/3600,2)
| eval recover_h=round((rec-con)/3600,2)
| table incident_id, detected_at, contained_at, recovered_at, contain_h, recover_h
description: We track detection, containment, eradication, and recovery timestamps from ITSM to evidence structured incident response progression.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes incident:milestone, incident:milestone`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Require milestone fields on CIP-tagged incidents. (2) Validate timestamps in UTC. (3) Dashboard for leadership. (4) Export for after-action reviews. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=incident:milestone`, `sourcetype=incident:milestone``.
visualization: Timeline (milestones), Table (SLAs), Column chart (mean contain hours)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for we track detection, containment, eradication, and recovery timestamps from itsm to evidence structured incident response progression. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=incident:milestone` or ServiceNow custom sourcetype.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require milestone fields on CIP-tagged incidents. (2) Validate timestamps in UTC. (3) Dashboard for leadership. (4) Export for after-action reviews.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=incident:milestone category="CIP008" earliest=-90d
| eval det=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval con=strptime(contained_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval rec=strptime(recovered_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval contain_h=round((con-det)/3600,2)
| eval recover_h=round((rec-con)/3600,2)
| table incident_id, detected_at, contained_at, recovered_at, contain_h, recover_h
```

Understanding this SPL

**Incident Response Timeline Milestone Tracking (CIP-008-6 R3 Part 3.2)** — We track detection, containment, eradication, and recovery timestamps from ITSM to evidence structured incident response progression.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=incident:milestone` or ServiceNow custom sourcetype. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: incident:milestone. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=incident:milestone, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **det** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **con** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **rec** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **contain_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **recover_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: con, contain_h, contained_at, det, detected_at, incident_id, rec, recover_h.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (milestones), Table (SLAs), Column chart (mean contain hours)
